tracking.controllers: Pimax Crystal Controllers or Lighthouse-compatible controllers

knownIssues.title: Pimax software can conflict with SteamVR
knownIssues.description: PiTool/Pimax Play sometimes conflicts with SteamVR updates, causing crashes or failed initialization.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep Pimax Play updated. If SteamVR crashes, restart Pimax Play service. Check Pimax forums for known compatibility issues with specific SteamVR versions.

knownIssues.title: Extremely GPU-demanding at native resolution
knownIssues.description: At 2880x2880 per eye (16.6M pixels per eye), the Crystal is one of the most demanding headsets on the market.
knownIssues.workaround: Use Pimax's Dynamic Foveated Rendering with eye tracking enabled. This can reduce pixel load by 40-50% in the periphery.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Dynamic Foveated Rendering with eye tracking
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Crystal can track where you're looking and focus its processing power there. This makes everything run much faster with barely noticeable quality loss.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: DFR reduces peripheral render resolution based on real-time eye tracking data. At 'Aggressive' setting, this reduces effective pixel fill by ~45%, bringing the Crystal's GPU requirements close to a standard 2K headset. Enable in Pimax Play → Settings → Smart Smoothing → DFR.

optimizationTips.tip: Use PimaxXR runtime for OpenXR games
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Using Pimax's own VR runtime instead of SteamVR can make games look better and run faster on the Crystal.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: PimaxXR runtime has lower overhead than SteamVR for OpenXR titles. Set PimaxXR as the active OpenXR runtime in Pimax Play. Note: SteamVR-only titles (non-OpenXR) still require SteamVR runtime.
